LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SCOTTSDALE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND UNDER CCP 430.10(D). EVEN IF THE INSURANCE POLICY PROVIDES FIRST PARTY COVERAGE TO PLAINTIFF, THE COMPLAINT INDICATES THAT JDS HAS THIRD PARTY COVERAGE. ALLOWING THE LITIGATION TO PROCEED AS PRESENTLY CONFIGURED VIOLATES EVID. CODE 1155. IN ADDITION, IT IS DUBIOUS WHETHER JDS AND DEFENDANT ARE PROPERLY JOINED TOGETHER BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST THEM DO NOT ARISE OUT OF THE SAME TRANSACTION. SEE CCP 379. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM AGAINST JDS ARISES FROM A DEFECTIVE PAINT JOB. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANT ARISES FROM ITS ALLEGED BREACH OF A CONTRACTUAL DUTY TO PROCESS/HONOR A FIRST PARTY INSURANCE CLAIM. WHATEVER CLAIMS PLAINTIFF MAY HAVE AGAINST DEFENDANT MUST BE PURSUED IN A SEPARATE ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
ORDER SUSTAINING DEFENDANT SCOTTSDALE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT LEVY DESIGN PARTNERS, INC.'S ("LDS") DEMURRER TO JDS BUILDERS GROUP'S ("JDS") CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART. AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS LDS HAS NOT CITED TO ANY APPLICABLE AUTHORITY DEMONSTRATING THAT A DUTY DOES NOT EXIST AS A MATTER OF LAW. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS JDS HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO CONSTITUTE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF. AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED. JDS IS GIVEN 10 DAYS LEAVE TO PLEAD FACTS, IF IT CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, WITH PARTICULARITY TO MEET THE HEIGHTENED PLEADING STANDARD FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT LEVY DESIGN PARTNERS, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT IS DENIED. CROSS-COMPLAINANT JDS BUILDERS GROUP HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS UNDER THE "TORT OF ANOTHER" DOCTRINE, WHICH ESTABLISHES GROUNDS TO RECOVER ATTORNEY'S FEES. (SEE PRENTICE V. NORTH AM. TITLE GUARANTY CORP., ALAMEDA DIVISION (1963) 59 CAL.2D 618, 620.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT LEVY DESIGN PARTNERS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. JDS BUILDERS GROUP, INC. (JDS) CANNOT SHOW ULTIMATE FACTS REQUIRED TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT REPRESENTATION. THE SPECIFICATIONS WERE NEITHER FACTUAL NOR POSITIVE ASSERTIONS. EVEN IF THE SPECIFICATIONS WERE REGARDED AS ASSERTIONS OF PAST OR EXISTING FACT, SUCH STATEMENTS WERE NOT MADE WITHOUT ANY REASONABLE GROUND FOR BELIEVING THEM TO BE UNTRUE. (SEE CIV. CODE, 1710, SUBD. 2; CIV. CODE, 1572, SUBD. 2; SMALL V. FRITZ COMPANIES, INC., (2003) 30 CAL. 4TH 167, 174). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ("SFUSD") HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THE PROBABLE VALIDITY OF ITS CLAIM. (CCP SEC. 484.090(A)(2).) THE ONLY EVIDENCE PRESENTED IN SUPPORT OF SFUSD'S MOTION IS THE DECLARATION OF ERIN HIRST, THE SENIOR PROJECT MANAGER. HIRST DECLARES THAT "INVESTIGATION BY EXPERT CONSULTANTS" REVEALED THAT DEFENDANT JDS BUILDERS GROUP, INC. ("JDS") FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE PROJECT SPECIFICATIONS, WHICH CAUSED SFUSD'S DAMAGES. (HIRST DECL., PARS. 6-7.) THE HIRST DECLARATION IS BASED ON HEARSAY, LACKS FOUNDATION, AND IS IMPERMISSIBLE EXPERT TESTIMONY ABOUT THE ULTIMATE CAUSE OF THE SFUSD'S DAMAGES. ADDITIONALLY, THE DECLARATION PROVIDED TO THE COURT AND OPPOSING COUNSEL DID NOT HAVE EXHIBITS B AND C ATTACHED. (SEE OPPOSITION BRIEF, FOOTNOTES 1 AND 2.) THEREFORE, SFUSD HAS NOT ESTABLISHED "IT IS MORE LIKELY THAN NOT" THAT THEY WILL OBTAIN A JUDGMENT AGAINST JDS. (GOLDSTEIN V. BARAK CONST. (2008) 164 CAL.APP.4TH 845, 852.) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976

Ntc Of Mtn To Amend Answer
Off Calendar Oct-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT JDS BUILDERS GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Amend Answer is Granted. No opposition filed. The Court signed the order submitted by email. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT
Off Calendar Sep-22-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT is Denied. Plaintiff San Francisco Unified School District ("SFUSD") has not established the probable validity of its claim. (CCP sec. 484.090(a)(2).) The only evidence presented in support of SFUSD's motion is the Declaration of Erin Hirst, the Senior Project Manager. Hirst declares that "investigation by expert consultants" revealed that Defendant JDS Builders Group, Inc. ("JDS") failed to comply with the project specifications, which caused SFUSD's damages. (Hirst Decl., pars. 6-7.) The Hirst Declaration is based on hearsay, lacks foundation, and is impermissible expert testimony about the ultimate cause of the SFUSD's damages. Additionally, the Declaration provided to the Court and opposing counsel did not have Exhibits B and C attached. (See Opposition Brief, footnotes 1 and 2.) Therefore, SFUSD has not established "it is more likely than not" that they will obtain a judgment against JDS. (Goldstein v. Barak Const. (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 845, 852.) Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

Off Calendar Apr-14-2014 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jun-10-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-08-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT LEVY DESIGN PARTNERS, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. JDS Builders Group, Inc. (JDS) cannot show ultimate facts required to state a cause of action for negligent representation. The specifications were neither factual nor positive assertions. Even if the specifications were regarded as assertions of past or existing fact, such statements were not made without any reasonable ground for believing them to be untrue. (See Civ. Code, 1710, subd. 2; Civ. Code, 1572, subd. 2; Small v. Fritz Companies, Inc., (2003) 30 Cal. 4th 167, 174). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-25-2014 Continued To Apr-30-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Continued To Mar-12-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross-Defendant Levy Design Partners, Inc.'s ("LDS") demurrer to JDS Builders Group's ("JDS") Cross-Complaint is overruled in part and sustained in part. As to the second and third causes of action, the demurrer is overruled as LDS has not cited to any applicable authority demonstrating that a duty does not exist as a matter of law. As to the fifth cause of action, the demurrer is overruled as JDS has alleged sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action for declaratory relief. As to the fourth cause of action, the demurrer is sustained. JDS is given 10 days leave to plead facts, if it can do so in good faith, with particularity to meet the heightened pleading standard for negligent misrepresentation. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-21-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT LEVY DESIGN PARTNERS, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT IS Denied. Cross-Complainant JDS Builders Group has pled sufficient facts under the "tort of another" doctrine, which establishes grounds to recover attorney's fees. (See Prentice v. North Am. Title Guaranty Corp., Alameda Division (1963) 59 Cal.2d 618, 620.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-25-2013 Continued To Jan-29-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SCOTTSDALE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS sustainED without leave to amend under CCP 430.10(d). Even if the insurance policy provides first party coverage to Plaintiff, the complaint indicates that JDS has third party coverage. Allowing the litigation to proceed as presently configured violates Evid. Code 1155. In addition, it is dubious whether JDS and Defendant are properly joined together because Plaintiff's claims against them do not arise out of the same transaction. See CCP 379. Plaintiff's claim against JDS arises from a defective paint job. Plaintiff's claim against Defendant arises from its alleged breach of a contractual duty to process/honor a first party insurance claim. Whatever claims Plaintiff may have against Defendant must be pursued in a separate action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
